task_completion.visual_evidence: The JStock application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Active market switched to United Kingdom
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active market indicator (usually a flag icon or text in the menu/status bar) shows the United Kingdom.
The agent types 'HSBA' or 'HSBC' into the stock search bar or stock dictionary dialog.
The ticker 'HSBA' (HSBC Holdings) appears as a row in the main JStock watchlist table.
The agent types 'BP' into the stock search bar or stock dictionary dialog.
The ticker 'BP' (BP plc) appears as a row in the main JStock watchlist table alongside HSBA.

integrity.description: Agent used the JStock GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, menu navigation, and typing within the JStock graphical interface, rather than terminal commands modifying CSV files directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual JStock application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being manipulated is clearly JStock (identifiable by its layout, menus, and icons), not a generic text editor or spreadsheet program.

integrity.description: No terminal scripts used to bypass GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing bash or Python scripts being executed to write 'HSBA' and 'BP' directly into the JStock configuration directories.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target market is the United Kingdom. The required stocks to be added are exactly two: 'HSBA' (HSBC Holdings) and 'BP' (BP plc). The VLM should verify these specific ticker symbols appear in the JStock watchlist under the UK market context.
